mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive equity returns only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or INDPRO gains fail to predict positive 20-day returns in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth signal broad economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate falls confirms genuine labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the divergence pattern fails to separate from baseline market drift.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production alongside falling retail sales indicate a synchronized supply-demand contraction, which forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during recessionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when the economy is in expansion, or the contraction pattern yields positive equity returns in over 50% of historical cases.
